commands.review_summary:
This PR bundles selected Node include-pattern test shards, adjusts Blacksmith runner sizing, and updates CI docs plus workflow guard tests.

PR surface: Tests +64, Docs 0, Config +15, Other +114. Total +193 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a CI automation PR rather than a user bug report. The relevant evidence is the planner/guard tests, Crabbox check:changed proof in the PR body, and the exact-head CI graph.
commands.review_followup: - Manual maintainer review is the next action because the PR is maintainer-labeled and the remaining decision is CI capacity risk acceptance, not a narrow repairable defect.

target.title: fix(ci): bundle test shards and right-size runners
target.body:
## Summary

- bundle only low-risk infra, cron, and command Node include-pattern shards into deterministic jobs capped at 64 test files, reducing the normal Node matrix from 95 planned shards to 63
- keep agents-core and gateway/server suites isolated after live validation showed shared mock state makes arbitrary bundling unsafe
- route lower-weight bundles and fixed lanes to 4 vCPU while retaining measured heavy suites on 8 vCPU
- right-size `build-artifacts` from 32 to 16 vCPU and Windows checks from 16 to 8 vCPU
- keep PR/main supersession cancellation enabled and document the runner allocation contract

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --reporter=dot test/scripts/ci-node-test-plan.test.ts test/scripts/ci-workflow-guards.test.ts`
- result: 2 files, 41 tests passed
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run -- env OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 corepack pnpm check:changed`
- Crabbox run: `run_68218ee27622` (exit 0)
- autoreview: clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- live CI run `27928007900`: 63 `checks-node-*` jobs materialized; no bundled gateway/agents-core failures. Remaining failures match current `main` baseline suites (`build-artifacts`, lint, extension bundle, agents-core/runtime/models, embedded, plugin SDK, core fast/tooling).
target.author: vincentkoc
